items.q: Does 'mestizaje' refer only to the mixing of languages?
items.correct: No - it denotes the mixing of peoples and cultures, especially European, indigenous and African heritage, central to Latin American identity.
items.misconception: Mestizaje just means Spanish borrowing words from indigenous languages.
items.random_wrong: Mestizaje is the name of a Mexican political party.
items.why: The concept covers ancestry, culture and identity; limiting it to language misses why thinkers like Vasconcelos made it the core of Latin American self-definition.

items.q: What does 'criollo' mean in the colonial and identity context?
items.correct: A person of European descent born in the Americas; 'mestizo' is the term for mixed European and indigenous heritage.
items.misconception: Criollo means a person of mixed race.
items.random_wrong: Criollo means a tourist visiting Latin America.
items.why: The colonial caste vocabulary is precise: confusing criollo with mestizo muddles discussions of independence movements and social hierarchy.

items.q: Is 'indio' the preferred modern term for indigenous peoples?
items.correct: No - 'pueblos originarios' or 'indígenas' are preferred; 'indio' is widely felt to be pejorative.
items.misconception: Indio is a neutral, standard term used in formal writing.
items.random_wrong: Indigenous peoples no longer exist in Latin America.
items.why: Terminology carries the history of discrimination; using respectful current terms is expected in essays on indigenismo and identity.
The answer examiners want is: Vasconcelos. “Mariategui” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
It's Mixed-race heritage. “Indigenous rights” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How do you say 'My sister is embarrassed' in Spanish?
items.correct: Mi hermana tiene vergüenza / está avergonzada.
items.why: Correct answer: Mi hermana tiene vergüenza / está avergonzada.. “Mi hermana es embarazo.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.correct: Could be his/her/your(formal)/their brother - context determines which.
items.why: It's Could be his/her/your(formal)/their brother - context determines which.. “It means 'a brother' (indefinite).” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which of these is the best definition of “Pueblos originarios”?
items.correct: Original peoples; modern term preferred over 'indios'.
items.misconception: Mixed-race heritage from European, indigenous, and African ancestry.
items.random_wrong: Person of European descent born in the Americas.
items.why: It's Original peoples; modern term preferred over 'indios'.. “Mixed-race heritage from European, indigenous, and African ancestry.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which of these is the best definition of “Mestizo”?
items.correct: Person of mixed European and indigenous heritage.
items.misconception: Person of European descent born in the Americas.
items.random_wrong: Peruvian writer (1911-1969) of Los rios profundos, key indigenista author.
items.why: Correct answer: Person of mixed European and indigenous heritage.. “Person of European descent born in the Americas.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which of these is the best definition of “Criollo”?
items.correct: Person of European descent born in the Americas.
items.misconception: Person of mixed European and indigenous heritage.
items.random_wrong: Cultural and political movement valuing indigenous peoples and their cultures.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Person of European descent born in the Americas.. “Person of mixed European and indigenous heritage.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which of these is the best definition of “Mestizaje”?
items.correct: Mixed-race heritage from European, indigenous, and African ancestry.
items.misconception: Original peoples; modern term preferred over 'indios'.
items.random_wrong: Person of European descent born in the Americas.
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Mixed-race heritage from European, indigenous, and African ancestry.. “Original peoples; modern term preferred over 'indios'.” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was La raza cosmica published?
items.why: It's 1925. “1960” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
